Journal.Title: Journal of applied research in intellectual disabilities : JARID

Article.ArticleTitle: Quality Considerations When Planning and Conducting a Virtual World Café Using an Inclusive Research Approach.

Abstract.AbstractText: There is a growing movement to equitably include people with intellectual and developmental disabilities in disability research, but traditional research systems pose challenges to sharing power and co-designing research with people with disabilities. We developed an Inclusive Research Learning Series (IRLS) to build the capacities of adults with disabilities to conduct research. Emerging Researchers (i.e., adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities participating in the IRLS) conducted a World Café to research support needs of disabled adults.
We scored the administration of the World Café using a pre-established rubric, and we qualitatively analysed semi-structured interviews with nine Emerging Researchers using deductive coding.
The inclusive research approach was successful in conducting a quality World Café. The Emerging Researchers shared considerations for inclusive research teams conducting future World Cafés.
We discuss considerations for those seeking to challenge barriers to research training opportunities for adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Kansas University Center on Disabilities, The University of Kansas, Lawrence, Kansas, USA.

Grant.Agency: Office of Special Education Programs, Office of Special Education and Rehabilitative Services

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: People with intellectual and developmental disabilities co‐led a high‐quality World Café research study. The Inclusive Research Learning Series prepared them to co‐lead a research study. Supporting materials, such as facilitator guides and scripts, helped the researchers with disabilities to do research. This article gives suggestions to researchers on how to do inclusive research.

Reference.Citation: Albrecht, J., A. L. Stark, E. Dongas, K. J. Wrona, and C. Dockweiler. 2022. “Hosting an Online World Café to Develop an Understanding of Digital Health Promoting Settings From a Citizen's Perspective—Methodological Potentials and Challenges.” International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health 19, no. 16: 9969. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph19169969.
